name: Camera Lock-on
description: A camera mode that frames a selected target while preserving enough control for movement and combat.
design_notes: Camera lock-on must coordinate target selection, camera framing, player orientation, and loss conditions. The system should feel stable without becoming sticky: players need to know which target is locked, why lock breaks, and how switching targets will behave in crowds.
design_purpose: Keep a chosen enemy or object visible during close-range action.
Support strafing, blocking, parrying, or orbiting around a selected target.
Reduce camera management load while preserving target-selection decisions.
player_fantasy: The player feels focused on one threat while still able to move and react around it.

parameters.name: lock_max_distance
parameters.description: Maximum distance at which a target can be acquired or retained.

parameters.name: lock_screen_center_weight
parameters.description: How strongly screen-space center position affects target priority.

parameters.name: line_of_sight_grace_ms
parameters.description: Occlusion grace before lock is broken.

parameters.name: target_switch_lockout_ms
parameters.description: Lockout window before another target cycle input is accepted.

parameters.name: orbit_framing_offset
parameters.description: Screen-space offset used while orbiting the locked target.

edge_cases: Large bosses may need multiple lock anchors rather than a single root transform.
Targets dying, teleporting, becoming invisible, or entering cutscenes should clear or retarget predictably.
Line-of-sight grace must handle pillars and brief occlusion without keeping lock through entire walls.
Split-screen and co-op cameras need per-player target ownership and UI markers.
common_bugs: Camera locks to a target pivot inside the mesh and clips into large enemies.
Stick drift repeatedly cycles targets because target switching lacks deadzone and cooldown.
Lock-on rotates the character but not attack traces, causing attacks to miss the locked target.
Occlusion checks hit the player or weapon and break lock incorrectly.
balancing_notes: Strong lock-on improves readability in duels but can reduce threat from flanking enemies.
Target switching should be predictable enough for crowded fights without automatically selecting the safest option.
accessibility_notes: Provide clear target indicators, reticle size options, and camera sensitivity controls for lock-on mode.
Offer reduced camera auto-rotation or slower orbit speeds for players sensitive to rapid camera movement.

implementation_notes.unity: Use a target candidate query plus Cinemachine target groups or a custom rig; track lock owner, anchor transform, occlusion grace, and switch cooldown in camera state.
implementation_notes.godot: Maintain a locked target NodePath and anchor offset, then blend Camera2D or Camera3D orientation toward the anchor while movement input still controls the player.
implementation_notes.unreal: Use PlayerCameraManager or a camera mode with Blackboard-like target state, and keep target switching, line traces, and UI markers authoritative per player.
implementation_notes.web: Store locked_target_id and target_anchor in camera state, score candidates in screen space, and interpolate camera heading while clamping player input authority.

example_games.title: The Legend of Zelda: Ocarina of Time
example_games.note: Uses Z-targeting to keep a selected enemy or object framed.

example_games.title: Dark Souls
example_games.note: Uses lock-on targeting for duels and melee positioning.
